pi_items.source: verifier.py strictly searches for these string substrings in the email body.
export_result.sh checks os.listdir for ".processed-tickets" case-insensitively, which corresponds to the "Processed-Tickets" folder name.
task_info.get("metadata") is used directly in verifier.py to extract these keywords, ensuring they are used for string matching in the archived email subjects.

privileged_info_summary: The task operates on real emails from the SpamAssassin Public Corpus. The specific email that triggers the keyword match is "ham_013.eml" (Subject: "[ILUG] Re: Problems with RAID1 on cobalt raq3"). The verifier checks that this email is forwarded to "tickets@localhost", its subject prefixed with "[TICKET]", and its body contains exactly "Priority: Normal" and "Tags: triage, bug". The agent must also archive the email in a folder named "Processed-Tickets".
